name: Supriya Sule
quote: Visited Jantar Mantar today to meet Shri Sonam Wangchuk ji and the students. Deeply concerned about Sonam Ji's health, I urged him to end his fast, as the country needs his voice and leadership for the long road ahead. This is not a political movement, it is about the future of our youth and the integrity of the NEET examination. It is deeply unfortunate that the Government has still not initiated a meaningful dialogue with the students. A democracy must respond with empathy, not silence. Standing in solidarity with Sonam Ji and every student seeking justice through peaceful and democratic means. Their concerns deserve to be heard with sincerity and resolved with compassion and fairness. This issue must be addressed with the seriousness it deserves, and I will continue to raise it in Parliament and every appropriate forum until the voices of our young people are heard.
